goal: Import a 142 MB customer STEP fixture using the real Isaac Sim CAD importer, check polycount/bounds, generate a separate simplified collision mesh.
thoughts: Real extension: omni.kit.asset_importer / CAD Importer. STEP goes through tesselation — result is meshes, NOT parametric CAD features. A 142 MB STEP at default tesselation can yield millions of triangles; scene_summary + bbox check before trusting. Collision mesh MUST be separate from render mesh (convex decomp or simplified geometry) — a 2M-face collision crashes PhysX. Don't suggest rebuilding from primitives — unrealistic for customer CAD.
code:
# 1. Confirm CAD importer shipping / extension name
lookup_knowledge(query="omni.kit.asset_importer Isaac Sim CAD importer STEP tesselation 5.0")

# 2. Import the STEP (GUI: File > Import > STEP, or scripted path below)
import_robot(
    urdf_path="<ABSOLUTE_STEP_PATH>",     # /home/kenji/cad/fixture_v7.step
    dest_path="/World/Cell/Fixture",
    fix_base=True,
    merge_fixed_joints=True
)

# 3. Sanity check polycount + bounds BEFORE trusting the result
scene_summary(stage_path="<STAGE>")
run_stage_analysis(scope="/World/Cell/Fixture")

# 4. Generate a simplified collision mesh separately from the render mesh
simplify_collision(
    prim_path="/World/Cell/Fixture",
    method="convex_decomposition",
    max_hulls="<N_HULLS>",
    voxel_resolution="<VOX>"
)

# Honest notes for Kenji:
#  - STEP import = tesselated meshes, not parametric features. Fillets survive as tris.
#  - Weekly customer updates: set up a scripted watch-folder re-import pipeline; not OOTB.
#  - Isaac Sim does not read STEP natively like VC. USD render path needs meshes.
#  - Collision mesh != render mesh. The CAD-beautiful render mesh would crash PhysX.
failure_modes: Using the render-mesh polycount as collision — 2M tris crashes PhysX simulate
Importing at mm without unit conversion — fixture appears 1000x oversized
Promising a native STEP reader without tesselation — it does not exist
No polycount sanity check — 142 MB STEP silently becomes multi-million-triangle stage
